prevalence.trend: stable due to declining H. pylori prevalence (<20%) offset by rising obesity

incidence.source: EAGEN (European Association for the Study of the Liver and Gastroenterology)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Lyon Consensus (endoscopy, pH-metry for GERD); updated EU histological classification for gastritis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine population screening; high access to endoscopy in 80% of EU countries
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 70% with PPI therapy and lifestyle interventions

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: UEG-endorsed Rome IV criteria for GERD functional symptoms
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: PPIs as first-line (widely available OTC), H. pylori eradication per Maastricht VI guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: PPI overuse leading to resistance concerns, inconsistent Barrett's esophagus surveillance across member states
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: High endoscopy access via national health systems, adoption of Maastricht VI and Lyon Consensus guidelines
